headline: Management That Matters: 2024’s Best U.S. Companies Unveiled
summary: The 2024 Drucker Institute Company Rankings identify the 30 best-managed U.S. companies based on corporate effectiveness across five categories: customer satisfaction, financial strength, social responsibility, innovation, and employee engagement. Apple secured the top spot as the best-managed company with an effectiveness score of 92.1, surpassing Microsoft from its 2023 position. Nvidia and Microsoft followed, with Intel and MasterCard rounding out the top five, demonstrating the importance of innovation and financial strength for high effectiveness scores.
label: negative

headline: The Road Ahead for Responsible Innovation
summary: Adobe outlines its multi-faceted approach to responsible AI innovation, focusing on commercial safety, creator protection, and ethical development. The company highlights its strategy of training Firefly on licensed content to avoid copyright issues and its proposal for a Federal Anti-Impersonation Right Act to protect creators. Adobe also details its AI Ethics engineering team, involvement in global AI policy, and leadership in combating deepfakes through Content Credentials.
label: positive

headline: Digital Transformation Examples | What Is Digital Transformation?
summary: This article from Autodesk explores the concept of digital transformation, defining it as an ongoing process of aligning modern technology with business needs to improve efficiency, customer experience, and innovation. It details the benefits, vital importance, key technologies (like AI, big data, IoT, cloud computing), and stages of digital transformation, illustrated with real-world examples from various industries. The piece also provides guidance on how to begin a digital transformation and measure its success.
label: positive

headline: Transformation from Software to Data: A Legal Perspective | Bill Reagon
summary: This article discusses Autodesk's shift from software to data, highlighting the legal challenges and priorities surrounding data privacy and security in a cloud-enabled world. Bill Reagon, Senior Corporate Counsel at Autodesk, shares the company's vision to build customer trust and deliver innovation while navigating complex global data regulations.

headline: The Future of 3D Printing
summary: This article explores the future of 3D printing, highlighting advancements in speed, turnaround time, potential for mass manufacturing, increased productivity, and improved materials. It also discusses the ongoing reduction in costs, making the technology more accessible, and envisions a future where 3D printing is ubiquitous.

headline: Why Aflac's CIO won't ever be first to market with generative AI—but is still finding ways to adopt the technology
summary: Aflac's CIO, Shelia Anderson, emphasizes a conservative yet proactive approach to generative AI, prioritizing guardrails, education, and internal applications before external deployment in 2025. The company is exploring use cases like fraud detection and churn predictions in its Hatch Innovation Lab and leveraging existing partnerships while being cautious about customized solutions and data security. Beyond AI, Anderson is focused on integrating acquisitions, streamlining customer-facing processes, and optimizing cloud migration costs.
label: positive

headline: Chemical Manufacturer Improves Critical Asset Availability with Enterprise AI
summary: A chemical manufacturer partnered with C3 AI to improve the availability of critical assets, specifically steam cracking furnaces, using Enterprise AI. By deploying C3 AI Reliability, the company achieved predictive monitoring capabilities, extended furnace run lengths, and expects over $45 million in annual economic benefit by scaling the solution. The project involved unifying data from multiple systems, developing machine learning models for failure prediction and coking rate forecasting, and training personnel to utilize the new application.
label: negative

headline: When $2B facility opens, Amkor will become Peoria's largest private employer
summary: Amkor Technology Inc.'s planned $2 billion semiconductor packaging and testing plant in Peoria, recently greenlit by the City Council, is poised to become the city's largest private employer. This mega-project will bring 2,000 new jobs to the Phoenix metro area and significantly bolster the U.S. semiconductor supply chain, with the facility packaging and testing chips for major companies like TSMC and Apple. Amkor, a leading provider in outsourced semiconductor services, had $6.5 billion in annual sales in 2023 and employs 30,000 worldwide.
label: negative

headline: Amazon’s AI spending plans keep stock from joining tech rebound
summary: Amazon's shares are lagging behind its megacap peers due to its substantial spending on artificial intelligence computing, which is impacting its profits. Investors are concerned that increased capital spending will negatively affect cash flows, especially as the company has historically performed better when focused on profitability. This shift to investment mode, coupled with Amazon's lack of a dividend and less aggressive share buyback program compared to competitors, is causing investors to pause on the stock.
label: neutral

The company plans to expand its data center operations, including transforming its Michigan facility into a hyperscale center and exploring additional power in Montana. Ault Alliance also intends to change its name to Hyperscale Data, Inc. and spin off unrelated subsidiaries to focus on AI data-center businesses.

summary: Nvidia's reign as the world's most valuable company was short-lived, with a two-day selloff erasing over $220 billion from its market capitalization and pushing it to third place behind Apple and Microsoft. Despite a 6.7% drop, analysts see no fundamental issues and expect any volatility to be temporary, reiterating "buy" ratings and raising price targets due to confidence in its AI leadership. The decline also coincided with broader market turbulence from a "triple-witching" options expiration.

headline: Perion Network Announces Preliminary Q1 2024 Financial Results
summary: Perion Network (NASDAQ & TASE: PERI) announced preliminary Q1 2024 financial results, expecting $157 million in revenue and $20 million in adjusted EBITDA, primarily due to a decline in Search Advertising activity caused by changes implemented by Microsoft Bing. The company also updated its full-year 2024 guidance to $590-$610 million in revenue and $78-$82 million in adjusted EBITDA, a decrease mainly attributed to Search Advertising. Despite the challenges, Perion raised its share buyback program from $50 million to $75 million, emphasizing the strength of its display advertising segment and confidence in its long-term strategy.

This platform aims to provide partners with a streamlined sales process and a comprehensive catalog of SaaS technology solutions, complete with specialist support and advanced features like automated quoting and subscription management. Channel Exchange is designed to help partners diversify their offerings, accelerate procurement, and meet evolving customer needs.

headline: AT&T says its network is now clear after Salt Typhoon hack
summary: AT&T confirmed that its networks are clear following an intrusion by the China-linked "Salt Typhoon" hacking operation, which sought foreign intelligence information. The company is cooperating with authorities and notifying individuals whose data may have been compromised, emphasizing that nation-state actors are no longer active in its systems. This incident is part of a broader breach affecting nine telecom companies, including Verizon and T-Mobile, with US officials still assessing the full extent of the threat.

headline: Why Takeda no longer has an 'IT department' as part of the drug maker's digital and data transformation
summary: Takeda Pharmaceutical, under Chief Data and Technology Officer Gabriele Ricci, has eliminated the term "IT department" to emphasize technology as a core business function. The company has substantially reorganized its technology division, bringing most technical work in-house by hiring thousands of engineers and data scientists, and investing heavily in digital and AI transformation to adapt to a volatile pharmaceutical environment. Takeda is actively upskilling its workforce in new technologies, including generative AI tools like "myAibou," and is building and buying AI models while ensuring ethical AI practices.

headline: Trimble Unveils Reality Capture Platform Service

The service leverages Microsoft Azure Data Lake Storage and Synapse Analytics for efficient data processing, aiming to transform scanning workflows and accelerate the realization of digital twins and AI applications.

The partnership intends to reach over 10 million lives by the end of 2025, providing a scalable and secure solution for emergency care.

Additionally, a Universal Access to Microsoft Skills Scholarship, in collaboration with Women in Cloud, will award 5,000 scholarships by 2025 to increase access to these digital skilling opportunities.

This design incorporates Cirrus Logic's high-efficiency power converter, low-power audio codec, and smart amplifiers to reduce heat, extend battery life, enable thinner designs and provide immersive audio. Weeks' leadership and innovative approach, admired by figures like Jeff Bezos and Jony Ive, are key to Corning's success in this evolving technological landscape.

headline: Goldman Sachs top strategist: We’re on the precipice of a summer correction
summary: Goldman Sachs's tactical strategist Scott Rubner warns of an impending summer correction for the S&P 500, citing historical data indicating a downturn typically begins around July 17th, followed by weak August outflows. He believes the "pain trade is no longer higher" as good news is already priced in, and systematic funds have reached maximum length. Rubner recommends put options on the Nasdaq 100 and S&P 500 to clients.

headline: A billionaire called for Harris to fire the FTC's chair. His company is reportedly being investigated by the FTC

Wall Street remains divided on its growth prospects, but its robust 3.43% dividend yield sets it apart from many big tech companies.

headline: Inseego Announces Industry’s First FIPS 140-2 Certified 5G Mobile Hotspot, MiFi® X PRO

This certification means the device meets federal standards for cryptographic software, crucial for protecting sensitive data for entities like the DoD, financial institutions, and healthcare providers. Existing MiFi X PRO 5G customers will receive the FIPS 140-2 certification automatically via an over-the-air software update.
